# edit-mode

Sub-plugin of `reorder`. Owns the user-facing edit-mode UI:

- A pen button on `Shell.Toolbar` (group `actions`) that toggles edit mode globally.
- A `Core.Root` contribution that listens for the Escape key and exits edit mode.

This plugin only depends on `@plugins/reorder/web` (for `setEditMode`/`useEditMode`) and `@plugins/shell/web` (for the toolbar slot). It is intentionally separate from the reorder primitive to avoid a cyclic import: shell's `slots.ts` wraps its slots with `Reorder.area`, so reorder cannot itself contribute to shell's slots.

<!-- AUTOGENERATED:BEGIN — do not edit; regenerated by `./singularity build` -->

## Plugin reference

- Description: Pen button on the top toolbar that toggles global edit mode for all reorderable slots; Esc exits edit mode.
- Web:
  - Contributes: `ActionBar.Item` → `PenButton`, `Shortcuts.Shortcut` "reorder.exit-edit-mode (escape)"
  - Uses: `primitives/icon-button.IconButton`, `primitives/shortcuts.defineShortcut`, `reorder.getEditMode`, `reorder.setEditMode`, `reorder.useEditMode`, `shell/action-bar.ActionBar`

<!-- AUTOGENERATED:END -->
